channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🚁 US Army Apache Helicopter Crashes Near Strait of Hormuz, Crew Rescued
item.description: A US Army Apache gunship went down near the strategic Strait of Hormuz waterway. President Trump confirmed both pilots are safe, though the cause remains unclear amid ongoing tensions with Iran in the region.

item.title: 🦠 Ebola Death Toll in DR Congo Climbs to 101
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo is fighting its 17th Ebola outbreak since the virus was first identified in 1976, with the death toll now exceeding 100 as health authorities struggle to contain the spread.

item.title: 📈 Investors See Tipping Point for Bonds After Geopolitics Shreds Old Playbooks
item.description: Bonds have failed to shield investors during the Iran-Israel conflict, but some fund managers believe debt markets may soon reclaim their safe-haven role if inflation begins to impact economic growth.

item.title: 🇦🇫 UN Urges Afghan Taliban to End Arrests of Women Over Dress Rules
item.description: The United Nations mission in Afghanistan expressed concern over the detention of women in a western province for allegedly failing to comply with dress requirements, urging Taliban authorities to treat all people equally.

item.title: 🏥 WHO Chief Urges Uganda to Reconsider Congo Border Closure Over Ebola
item.description: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said Uganda should reconsider its decision to close its border with the Democratic Republic of Congo due to the ongoing Ebola outbreak.

item.title: ✈️🇪🇺 France and Germany cancel €100 billion FCAS joint fighter jet program
item.description: Europe's most ambitious collaborative defense program has been scrapped after France and Germany failed to resolve disputes over intellectual property and work sharing between Airbus and Dassault Aviation. The cancellation comes as Western military leaders warn of growing threats from Russia.

item.title: 🌊🌍 UN report warns of deepening crisis in world's oceans
item.description: A major UN assessment by 600 scientists found oceans warming and rising faster than ever, with 16% of ocean heat increase since 1955 occurring since 2018. Sea level rise has more than doubled to 4.3mm per year, as marine ecosystems approach critical tipping points.

item.title: 🇲🇲🇮🇩 Indonesia reaffirms Myanmar as ASEAN family in visit to junta leader
item.description: Indonesia's Foreign Minister Sugiono met Myanmar's junta chief-turned-president Min Aung Hlaing, delivering President Prabowo's message that Myanmar remains part of the ASEAN family. The visit comes two months after the general who ousted an elected government was sworn in as president.

item.title: 🇦🇪🇮🇷 UAE deepens alignment with US bloc despite conciliatory rhetoric on Iran
item.description: Analysis shows the UAE is distinguishing itself as the GCC's most hawkish member on Iran, even as other Gulf states pursue diplomatic engagement with Tehran. The Emirates are deepening their alignment with the American bloc despite recent conciliatory comments.

item.title: 🕊️ Iran and Israel halt military operations after 48-hour exchange of fire
item.description: Both countries called a halt to military operations after the most intense exchange of fire in months. Irans president said the country had not abandoned peace negotiations despite the escalation.

item.title: ⚖️ ICC prosecutor Karim Khan suspended amid sexual misconduct investigation
item.description: The International Criminal Courts chief prosecutor has been suspended while member states consider disciplinary measures, marking a major crisis for the tribunal investigating war crimes in Ukraine and Gaza.

item.title: ⛽🇺🇦 Kremlin admits fuel supply problems after Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy sites
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov acknowledged certain problems with fuel supplies in southern Russia after a surge in Ukrainian airstrikes on Russian energy and fuel infrastructure disrupted distribution.

item.title: ⚔️ Israel and Iran Halt Strikes After Trump Warning
item.description: Israel and Iran paused military strikes following a direct appeal from US President Donald Trump, who warned Netanyahu that Israel would be "on your own" if it resumed attacks. The temporary ceasefire comes after months of conflict that has disrupted global oil markets.

item.title: ☢️ IAEA Urges Iran to Re-Engage on Nuclear Inspections
item.description: UN nuclear watchdog chief Rafael Grossi called on Iran to allow inspections at sites bombed by the US and Israel. The US led a push for a resolution at the IAEA board demanding Iran resume cooperation.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Edge Higher Amid Iran-Israel Uncertainty
item.description: Oil prices rose slightly as markets awaited clarity on whether the Iran-Israel halt would hold. The Strait of Hormuz remains a focal point, with Iran's envoy saying transit fees would apply.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanon Says Israel Bombed It Nearly 3,500 Times During Ceasefire
item.description: Israel conducted nearly 3,500 airstrikes on Lebanon since the April ceasefire announcement, Lebanon's defense minister said. Lebanese President Aoun appealed directly to Israel to pursue negotiations instead of war.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 Xi Jinping Visits North Korea for First Summit in Seven Years
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang for his first visit in seven years, meeting with Kim Jong Un. The summit follows Putin's recent visit and signals deepening coordination between China and North Korea.

item.title: 🇲🇾 Malaysia opposition in turmoil as Islamist party PAS cuts ties with Bersatu
item.description: PAS ended its cooperation with Bersatu, gutting the Perikatan Nasional opposition coalition ahead of key elections in Malaysia.

item.title: 🌏 Asian stocks track Wall Street tech bounce as oil eases on Mideast ceasefire hopes
item.description: Asian markets rebounded as investors returned to AI stocks while crude prices eased following Israel-Iran hostilities pause.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea Names First Female Prime Minister in Decades to Lead AI Push
item.description: President Lee Jae Myung nominated former Naver CEO Han Seong-sook as prime minister, tasking her with driving South Korea's AI-driven economic transformation.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Prepares 21st Sanctions Package Against Russia Worth $1.5 Trillion
item.description: The European Union is finalizing its 21st sanctions package against Russia, targeting additional sectors to increase economic pressure on Moscow.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 Xi and Kim Agree to Expand Cooperation at Pyongyang Summit
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and North Korean leader Kim Jong Un held a rare summit in Pyongyang, agreeing to expand cooperation in politics, economy and culture.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Blames US for Latest Exchanges of Fire with Israel
item.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry says overnight strikes will worsen an already chaotic diplomatic process with Washington, exchanging messages in an atmosphere of extreme suspicion.

item.title: 🇵🇱 Poland Seeks Calm with Ukraine Over WWII Army Unit Dispute
item.description: Polish PM Tusk calls for solidarity and talks after diplomatic relations deteriorated over Ukraine naming an army unit for nationalist insurgents who massacred Poles in World War Two.

item.title: 📉 Gulf Stock Markets Drop Amid Renewed Middle East Tensions
item.description: Most stock markets in the Gulf ended lower on Monday as renewed conflict in the Middle East weighed on investor sentiment.
